summary: US forces have launched multiple self‑defence strikes against air‑defence, radar and command sites in southern Iran, prompting Iranian air‑defence activation and explosions reported across Bandar Abbas, Qeshm, Minab and Sirik. Iran’s IRGC has declared the Strait of Hormuz closed to all vessels and warned of a decisive response to any further US aggression. Israel continues operations against Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon while Iranian‑backed attacks have struck an American base in Erbil, Iraq.
key_points: US CENTCOM initiated self‑defence strikes on multiple targets in southern Iran, including air‑defence systems, radars and UAV command units (Source: @VahidOnline/76170).
Iranian sources reported explosions and activation of air‑defence in Bandar Abbas, Qeshm, Minab, Sirik and other southern locations (Source: @mamlekate/103656).
IRGC announced the immediate closure of the Strait of Hormuz to all vessels, threatening to target any transit (Source: @shin_persian/6901).
Hezbollah‑linked infrastructure in southern Lebanon, including rocket launchers and drone launch sites, was targeted by IDF strikes (Source: @idf_telegram/24265).
An Iranian missile reportedly struck the US Al‑Harir base in Erbil, Iraq, and an American radar in the same city was destroyed (Source: @SharghDaily/165163).
US aircraft F‑16 operating over the Persian Gulf was engaged by IRGC missile fire, which the jet evaded (Source: @PressTV/193573).
Multiple US statements describe the operations as “self‑defence” and indicate hundreds of targets near the Hormuz Strait will be hit (Source: @pkpoi/142669).
Israeli officials claim Israel is not involved in the US attacks on Iran and that Israeli forces remain prepared for high‑intensity action against Iran (Source: @inon_yttach/8861).

sections.executive_summary: US CENTCOM has begun self‑defence strikes on numerous southern Iranian targets, prompting Iranian air‑defence activation and explosions across multiple coastal cities. Iran’s IRGC has closed the Strait of Hormuz and warned of a decisive response, while Israeli forces continue attacks on Hezbollah sites in southern Lebanon. Regional tensions are heightened by Iranian missile attacks on a US base in Erbil and engagements with US aircraft over the Persian Gulf.

points: Multiple explosions were heard in Bandar Abbas, Qeshm, Minab, Sirik and the island of Haimah, with locals reporting missile‑like sounds followed quickly by blasts, indicating rapid strike‑to‑impact times (Source: @mamlekate/103656).
Iranian air‑defence systems were activated in the south, including radar and missile batteries, as reported by local sources following the explosions (Source: @naya_foriraq/78241).
The IRGC announced the immediate closure of the Strait of Hormuz to all vessels, stating any transit would be targeted (Source: @shin_persian/6901).
Iranian officials warned of a “harsh and decisive response” to any new aggression, emphasizing readiness for expanded ground operations (Source: @VahidOnline/76161).
An Iranian missile strike hit the US Al‑Harir base in Erbil, Iraq, and destroyed a US radar there, marking a cross‑border escalation (Source: @SharghDaily/165163).
IRGC forces fired a missile at a US F‑16 over the Persian Gulf, which evaded the attack (Source: @PressTV/193573).

The Israeli Defence Forces reported continued strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon, targeting rocket launchers, drone launch sites and ammunition depots in the city of Zoor and surrounding areas (Source: @idf_telegram/24265).

Israeli officials stated that Israel is not involved in the US attacks on Iran and that the Israeli military remains prepared for high‑intensity operations against Iran if needed (Source: @inon_yttach/8861).
Israel’s annual security award highlighted a unique weapons system that played a central role in the campaign against Iran, though no operational details were disclosed (Source: @inon_yttach/8860).
Israeli media cited statements that the campaign against Iran is far from over and that the IDF is ready to strike Iran with great force (Source: @inon_yttach/8857).
A senior Israeli source reported that Israel currently has no role in the US‑led attacks on Iran (Source: @SharghDaily/165170).
No specific Gaza or West Bank operational reports were present in the source material; therefore no actionable points can be extracted.
IDF statements confirmed strikes on Hezbollah‑linked rocket launchers and drone launch infrastructure in southern Lebanon, including six sites in Zoor (Source: @idf_telegram/24265).
Hezbollah’s activities were noted as ongoing, with Israeli forces targeting ammunition stores and prepared launchers over the past 24 hours (Source: @idf_telegram/24274).
Israeli officials warned that Hezbollah’s continued aggression could prompt further Israeli operations in the border region (Source: @almogboker78/17444).
Iran launched a missile at the US Al‑Harir base in Erbil, Iraq, and destroyed a US radar in the same city, indicating a direct strike on US assets in Iraq (Source: @SharghDaily/165163).
US CENTCOM announced additional self‑defence strikes against multiple targets in Iran, describing them as a response to ongoing Iranian aggression (Source: @shin_persian/6896).
US officials described the current wave of attacks as “self‑defence” and indicated that hundreds of targets near the Hormuz Strait will be engaged (Source: @pkpoi/142669).
US CENTCOM forces launched strikes against multiple Iranian targets, including air‑defence, radar and UAV command units, as part of a self‑defence campaign (Source: @VahidOnline/76170).
US officials stated that the attacks are “strong and clear” and that the US Central Command will conduct “hard hits” on key Iranian facilities (Source: @VahidOnline/76164).
US Central Command reported that at 5:15 p.m. ET additional self‑defence strikes were ordered against several Iranian targets (Source: @shin_persian/6896).
US statements emphasized that the operations are a response to Iran’s “unprovoked and ongoing aggression” (Source: @shin_persian/6896).

key_developments: US CENTCOM launched self‑defence strikes on multiple southern Iranian air‑defence, radar and UAV command sites, marking the first coordinated US offensive in the region (Source: @VahidOnline/76170).
Iranian air‑defence systems were activated across Bandar Abbas, Qeshm, Minab and Sirik following reported explosions, indicating heightened alert status (Source: @mamlekate/103656).
The IRGC declared the Strait of Hormuz closed to all vessels and warned that any transit would be targeted, escalating maritime tension (Source: @shin_persian/6901).
Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon, including rocket launchers and drone launch sites, was struck by IDF forces, continuing the Israel‑Hezbollah front (Source: @idf_telegram/24265).
Iran fired a missile at a US F‑16 over the Persian Gulf, which evaded the attack, demonstrating direct air‑to‑air engagement (Source: @PressTV/193573).
An Iranian missile struck the US Al‑Harir base in Erbil, Iraq, and destroyed a US radar there, extending the conflict into Iraq (Source: @SharghDaily/165163).
US officials described the ongoing attacks as “self‑defence” and announced that hundreds of targets near the Hormuz Strait will be engaged in the coming hours (Source: @pkpoi/142669).
sections.threat_assessment: The United States is conducting a sustained air campaign against Iranian air‑defence and command infrastructure in the south, while Iran is responding with missile strikes on US assets in Iraq and aggressive air‑defence actions over the Persian Gulf. Iran’s closure of the Strait of Hormuz threatens global oil shipping and raises the risk of naval confrontation. Israel’s continued strikes on Hezbollah in southern Lebanon add another active front, potentially drawing Lebanese forces into broader hostilities.
sections.regional_response: Iranian authorities have mobilized air‑defence units and issued public warnings of decisive retaliation, while the IRGC’s closure of the Hormuz Strait signals a willingness to target commercial shipping. US officials maintain that operations are defensive, but the scale of strikes suggests an escalation beyond limited retaliation. Israel’s statements of readiness to strike Iran and ongoing attacks on Hezbollah indicate a coordinated, multi‑theater posture among regional actors.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is critical as Iranian threats could impact commercial vessels. Continued SIGINT on Iranian missile launches toward US bases in Iraq will help anticipate further cross‑border strikes. Israeli and US coordination on targeting Iranian assets should be tracked to assess the risk of broader escalation, especially if Hezbollah escalates attacks from Lebanon.
